title: Lampropappus eremanthifolia

It is classified as a member of the genus Lampropappus, which is a part of the tribe Gnaphalieae. The species Lampropappus eremanthifolia is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its yellow flowers. This plant is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. The classification of Lampropappus eremanthifolia is a key aspect of understanding its unique characteristics and distribution.
